item.title: AI disruption fear might strike auto insurers next. Two stocks are in the crosshairs
item.description: Artificial intelligence could shake up car insurance stocks as autonomous vehicles become more ubiquitous.

item.title: These stocks can insulate investors from AI risk — and they pay dividends, says Jenny Harrington
item.description: Dividend stocks are outperforming this year.

item.title: Anxious investors should buy these two telco stocks for safety, says analyst
item.description: Daiwa Capital Markets sees 21% upside for shares of Verizon and 13% for T-Mobile.

item.title: Charts suggest this sector could be the next to lead the market higher
item.description: As the market has hit new milestones, the breadth has been strong, but the leadership is wrong, Jay Woods says.

item.title: Walmart shares recover after initial drop post-earnings. Here's why
item.description: Walmart shares ticked up 2% on Thursday as analysts noted that the retailer appeared to be sandbagging forward guidance.
